- Ech si net Kinoskeck.
- Wann ech rauskin, dann am léifsten an de Kino.
- Mä frënn, ech woch heinsto e Läng.
- Dat ass de Plaatzt um Kino.
- Et brauch ee keen fir matzegoen.
- Et ass super, datt Filmer hei all an der Originalversioun lafen.
- Ech kucken alles, vun Actionfilmer bis romantesch Liebesfilmer.
- Och Filmer an 3D kucken ech gär.
- Schlussde Brüll gëtt mer se op Nerven.
- Wat mech awer am Kino steiert, ass, datt Leit do heinsto mengen, si wären an engem Restaurant.
- Popcorn oder Schipp jässe mecht ziemlech viel Kamedie,
- an dat nervt.
- An nerser dem, riecht et net gutt.
- I am not a cinema snob.
- When I go out, I prefer the cinema.
- But friend, I sometimes get a long wait.
- That is the place at the cinema.
- It does not take anyone to join me.
- It is great that films here run all in their original version.
- I watch everything, from action films to romantic love stories.
- I also like watching films in 3D.
- The loudest noise is getting on my nerves.
- What, however, annoys me at the cinema, is that people there think they are in a restaurant.
- Popcorn or chips eating makes quite a mess,
- and that is annoying.
- And besides, it does not smell good.

- Ech hunn en Abonnement fir an den Theater.
- Ech hunn de Programm selwer zesummegestallt, an dofir ass en immens variéiert.
- Vu klenge Produktiounen, iwwert Operen bis zum Ballet, ech hunn alles gären.
- Präisser, zemol sou en Abonnement, sinn och zimlech sozial, an de Virdel ass, datt een ëmmer eng gutt Plaz huet.
- Leider muss ech dacks eleng goen.
- Mäi Frënd huet keen Abonnement a kritt net ëmmer eng Plaz.
- Wann e Steg an der Woch um Programm ass, ass et natierlech och heiansdo ustrengend, no engem laangen Aarbechtsdag nach iwwert dräi Stonnen am Theater ze sëtzen a moies nees fréi opzestoen.
- I have a subscription for the theater.
- I put together the program myself, and therefore it is immensely varied.
- From small productions to operas to ballet, I like everything.
- Prices, especially such a subscription, are also quite social, and the advantage is that you always have a good seat.
- Unfortunately, I often have to go alone.
- My friend does not have a subscription and does not always get a seat.
- If there is a show in the week on the program, it is naturally sometimes exhausting to sit in the theater for more than three hours after a long day of work and get up early again in the morning.

- No enger langer Schaffwoch kënne Fräides oder Samschdes Owes d'Sonskier mam menger Mamm an der Restaurant iessen.

- Freideg owes huet mech gemaach.
- Mir ginn natierlech bowlen, mat menge Kollegen aus der Schoul.
- Et fréier mir schonn den ganzen Owend drop.
- Mir hunn als Kanner sa wéiat als schobal profien, awer nëmme bal.
- Dat ass natierlech och geléngt fir alt Kolleegen erëm ze gesinn.
- An nom Aarbecht an Bowle gëtt natierlech och e Patt gedronk.
- Ech muss awer nach bowlinge schoen kafe.
- Déi geléinte sinn net sou agreabel.
- Friday evening wore me out.
- We are naturally going bowling with my school friends.
- We have been looking forward to it all evening.
- As kids, we were as eager as soon as we could, but only almost.
- It also naturally succeeded in seeing old colleagues again.
- After work and bowling, a drink is naturally also had.
- But I still have to buy bowling shoes.
- The rented ones are not so comfortable.

- Sieben, ech interesséieren mech immens fir Musek.
- Ech kéint den ganzen Dag Musek lauschteren, mee net onbedéngt klassesch Musek, léiwer Rock, op alle Fall modern.
- Do fir, wou ech Konzerter esou immens gär hunn, sinn mir dann an der Rockhall, am Atelier oder och an engem klengen Concertssall.
- Leider sinn d'Konzerter awer net geschenkt, an e groussen Deel vu mengem Budget geet och drop, well ech bal all Weekend op engem Concert sinn.
- Seven, I am very interested in music.
- I could listen to music all day, but not necessarily classical music, I prefer rock, at least modern.
- So where I love concerts so much, I am in the Rock Hall, the Atelier, or even in a small concert hall.
- Unfortunately the concerts are not free, and a large part of my budget goes on that as I am at a concert almost every weekend.

- Wat dus du weegend machen?
- Dommesdinn zierlich.
- A mengem Lieblingsclub lees all Samschdes en DJ op.
- Ech raafe meng Frënn a Frëndinnen, an das richtig cool.
- Meestens geet et nur hollef nüchst, recht richtig lass, an dort geet et bis an de freie Moien.
- Ech mengen, ech brauch es net ze soen, dat meng Sonndescher ech da ruis verlafwen.
- Wann ech fir en Examen lehrungs, kann ech naturlisch net lompe goen.
- What are you going to do this weekend?
- Dommesdinn zierlich.
- At my favorite club, every Saturday a DJ plays.
- I call my friends, and that’s really cool.
- Most of the time it’s just half-heartedly, very properly left, and then it goes until the early morning.
- I think I don’t need to say it, that my Sunday shirt briefly falls apart here.
- When I’m studying for an exam, of course I can’t skip going.

- Guckt man ich fast aus jonskalender an der gleiche Rubrik Agenda vum Radio.
- Iwwert waeht dir Evenementer aus dem Manifestationskalender Schwaase si och am Agenda.
- Mach keeät déi Evenementer.
- Dat wäert für de Sport.
- Bleift nach den Agenda von desem Weekend.
- Karin, wat ass de Weekend los?
- Ja, wie immer ass ganz viel as am Menschen.
- Hei si just de profilott Evenementer erausgepickt.
- Haut den Ovent an sam Kader vum Litze-Boeja Literaturfestival eng Liesung mat direkt drei litze-Boeja Autoren an drei Sprochen.
- Dat ass am Kaffee Dix um halver Aacht geht et los.
- Dann hann mer narapess für Theaterfrön.
- Ooft den Ovent um Aachdauer könnt ihr am Studio vum Neien Theater e ganz speziell Steck gesehen, an zwar stiehnei Münsche man enge Behinderung op der Bühne.
- Regie feiert Martin Müller, die selwer am Rotstuhl sitzt.
- Für Frön vum Tanz ass samstäsch den Ovent um halver Aacht am Ascher Theater Premier von engem ganz dynamischen Tanzspektakel, wonn ut nomen Tänzer, mi oft Musiker op der Bühne stien.
- A wenn et Leber sportlich wird, kann von Aachdauer und final von der Fussball-Europameisterschaft um grössen Ekron um Knüedler kucken.
- Fehler soll jo gutt gehen, also vierwert net.
- Detailer a weitere Evenementer fand ihr wie immer an eine Rubrik Agenda op eiserem Sied.
- Merci Karin.
- Da komme ma elo bei het viada.
- De Weekend präsentiertet sich ganz zonne...
- Look at me, almost from Jonskalender in the same section Agenda of the radio.
- Across which events from the Manifestationskalender Swiss are also in the Agenda.
- Do not include those events.
- That will be for the sports.
- Stay with the Agenda for this weekend.
- Karin, what is happening this weekend?
- Yes, as always there is a lot of people.
- Here they are just the profile events picked out.
- Tonight the event in the context of the Litze-Boeja Literature Festival a reading with directly three Litze-Boeja authors and three languages.
- That is at Kaffee Dix at half past seven it starts.
- Then we have narapess for theater friends.
- Often the event at eight could you in the studio of the New Theater see a very special feature, namely Münsche man with a disability on the stage.
- Direction celebrates Martin Müller, who sits in the red chair himself.
- For friends of dance on Saturday the event at half past eight at Ascher Theater premiere of a very dynamic dance spectacle, where from the dancer, with often musicians on stage sit.
- And when it becomes more sporty, you can watch the eight and final of the Fußball-Europameisterschaft at the biggest Ekron on Knueder.
- The errors should go well, so don’t worry.
- Details and more events you will find as always in an Agenda section on our site.
- Thank you, Karin.
- Now we come to the next.
- The weekend presents itself quite sunny...

- Wäsch dat Kommentarer vu vier Leit, déi de Weekend op enger vun dëne Manifestatioune waren.
- Schreiwt soelen eent bis zweeër bei den echtesch Manifestatiounen ama Gender.
- Eent
- Ech war samschtisch den Owend um Bellwalfieen uewet's Raleigh.
- Dat war super!
- Fir noets mat flotte Frouen iwwer de ganze Sitt geschäkt.
- Ech fanne de Sitt jo sowiesou immens, mä mam Déchbitzeller beliicht do war dat nach besser.
- Mir sinn och bis Uewen op een vun den Häischiew geklommen.
- Also do ass et mir zwar e bëssi komisch ginn.
- Ech war drun, Täisch, Jünett.
- Zum Schluss gouf et nach en herrleche Spektakel mat zwärescher Musek rundrem Täischiewen.
- Bon, ech muss soen, et ass zimlech spéit ginn.
- Um hallef Nuisch krug och nach Präisverdilung an der Rokal.
- Mir ware bëssen enttäuscht, dat mer net den éischte Präis gewonnen hunn, mä et war super, dat mer mattgemach hunn.
- Wash the comments from four people who were at one of those demonstrations this weekend.
- Write one to two lines about the actual demonstrations about gender.
- One
- I was in the evening at Bellwalfieen up near Raleigh.
- That was great!
- To-night with fancy women all around the site shaken.
- I find the site anyway incredibly, but with the debris clear that was even better.
- We also climbed up to one of the houses up.
- So there it is a little strange to me.
- I was there, Täisch, Jünett.
- In the end there was still a splendid spectacle with whips of music around Täischiewen.
- Well, I must say, it got quite late.
- At half night I also got prize distribution in the Rokal.
- We were a bit disappointed that we did not win the first prize, but it was great that we participated.

- It was a fast evening, but I was relieved that it all went off without a major täschefall.

- Lies allerschte dem Massimo säi Portrait.
- Ma kee hat mat zo verschiedene Faarwen, wat hie gemmercht a wat hie net gemmercht.
- De Massimo ass viru véier Méint op Lëtzebuerg komm.
- Hie kënnt aus enger klenger italienescher Stad bei Roum.
- Hie nachs net bestuet a wunnt eleng.
- Hie schafft fir eng italienesch Firma als Kommerzial um Kierchbierg a reest zimmlech vill fir seng Aarbecht.
- Dat gefält him ganz gutt.
- Wann hien zu Lëtzebuerg ass, fiert hie vill mam Vëlo oder hie geet an de Bësche lafen.
- Beim Laftrëff huet hien och schonn Leit kenne geléiert.
- Um Büro huet hie eng Fussballsequipe.
- Dat géif hie nach interesséieren.
- Fussball war nach emmer säi gréissten Hobby.
- Wann owes kee vu senge Bekannten oder Kollegen Zäit huet, bleift hie nëmmen doheem, well hien net gär eleng an e Café geet.
- Heiansdo geet hien awer an de Kino.
- Am léifste kuckt hie lëschteg Filmer.
- Invitatoune fir an den Theater ze goen, huet hie bis elo emmer refuséiert, datt ass wierklech net senges.
- Zënter zwee Méint ass hien och an engem Sproochecours fir e bësse Lëtzebuergesch ze léieren.
- Dee Cours mëcht him richteg vill Spaass.
- Hien huet och do schonn eng Partie Leit aus der ganzer Welt begéint.
- Read Massimo's portrait first.
- But who has with so many different colors, what he did and what he did not do.
- Massimo came to Luxembourg four months ago.
- He comes from a small Italian town near Rome.
- He is not yet married and lives alone.
- He works for an Italian company as a commercial at the Kierchbierg and travels quite a lot for his work.
- He likes it very much.
- When he is in Luxembourg, he rides his bike a lot or he goes into the woods to run.
- At Laftrëff he has already learned to know people.
- At the office he runs a football team.
- He would still be interested in that.
- Football has always been his biggest hobby.
- If in the evening none of his friends or colleagues have time, he stays at home, because he does not like to go alone to a café.
- Sometimes he goes to the cinema.
- He prefers to watch funny movies.
- Invitations to go to the theater, he has so far always refused, that is really not his thing.
- For two months he is also in a language course to learn a little Luxembourgish.
- That course really makes him a lot of fun.
- He has already met a lot of people from all over the world there.

- Ausser ech gesinn, datt meng Hëllef erwënscht ass.
- When I first invited friends home here, it was normal for me to help them clean up after the meal and games.
- But I was the only one who got up, drank alcohol, and emptied the yogurt.
- In the kitchen there was also quite a bit to Janine and I think I disturbed her a little there.
- Next time only set the yogurt.
- Unless I see that my help is desired.

- Notéiert déi verschidden Iddien fir e Kado an de Grond,
- firwat si dat awer net matzuelen.
- Read this situation and listen to the dialogue.
- Note the different ideas for a gift and the reason why they do not accompany it.

column1:
- Liest dës Situatioun a lauschtert den Dialog.
- Notéiert déi verschidden Iddien fir e Kado an de Grond,
- firwat si dat awer net matzuelen.
column2:
[sound:inll_1badd5e22ef3_B1_KAPITEL_2_B1_AUDIO_49_001.mp3]
- Read this situation and listen to the dialogue.
- Note the different ideas for a gift and the reason why they do not accompany it.

- So, wüst du gesinn?
- Mir haten eng Invitatioun vu Monika a vum Peter an de Bréifbréif.
- Nee, dat hun ech nach net gesin. Wie war dat dann?
- Dat ass eng Invitatioun fir eng Grillparty, dis e Sammestisch den Oowend bei hinnen doheem.
- Gimmer!
- Oh, flott! Um wéi vill Auer fänkt se dann un?
- Um éischt zéng Auer.
- Gut, dat klappt. Da musse mir lo just nach iwwerluegen, wat mir denen z'ëmaachen.
- Wat heils du vun enger schéiner Kerz oder enger flotter Korb?
- Also, an der Invitatioun steet, dat si näischt matbréngen.
- Gär nees!
- Ech ginn awer wierklech net gär mat Eedelen hëllefen hinne.
- A wat mir da géingen eng Salat oder en Dessert matbréngen?
- Hei kuck, se schreiwen, vergiessen a Dréngendes ass gesuergt.
- Hm, jo, dat geet dann net.
- Dat wär onhöflech, oder?
- A mir wësse jo och net, wat gegrillt gëtt, an da passt et eventuell net bei déi aner Saachen.
- Jo, dat gesäit een esou.
- Mee wanns de wierklech onbedéngt eppes matbréngen wëlls, wat heils du dann vun enger Kéisknippchen?
- Nee, dat geet net.
- De Pit huet mir déi lescht Kéier erzielt, datt hien probéiert seng Ernierung ëmzestellen.
- Hien wëllt elo manner Zocker a manner Kohlenhydrater iessen.
- Ok, dat si Kéisknippchen wierklech keng gutt Iddi.
- Vläicht du eng schéin Bukett?
- Oh nee, du weess dach, datt Monika eng Allergie op verschidde Schnëttblummen huet.
- Oh mei, do muss ech mech geduecht hunn.
- Mee da komme mir eben an eng Bëscherboutique.
- Dat ass ëmmer gutt.
- Jo, dat ass eng gutt Iddi.
- Da kënne si sech eraussichen, wat si wëllen.
- Tipptopp, also maachen mir et.
- So, you want to see it?
- We received an invitation from Monika and Peter in the mail.
- No, I haven't seen that yet. What was it then?
- That's an invitation for a barbecue, a gathering at their place in the evening.
- Great!
- Oh, nice! What time does it start?
- At ten o'clock.
- Okay, that works. Then we just have to figure out what to bring.
- What would you think about a nice candle or a nice basket?
- So, the invitation says they bring nothing.
- Gladly not!
- But I really don't like helping noble people.
- And what would we bring, a salad or a dessert?
- Look, they write, forgetfulness and urgency are taken care of.
- Hmm, yes, that won't do.
- That would be rude, right?
- And we don't know what will be grilled, and then it might not go with the other things.
- Yes, that makes sense.
- But if you really must bring something, what would you bring from a cheese nibble?
- No, that won't do.
- Pit told me last time that he is trying to redesign his diet.
- He wants to eat less sugar and fewer carbohydrates now.
- Okay, cheese nibble really isn't a good idea.
- Maybe you could bring a nice bouquet?
- Oh no, you know Monika has an allergy to several cut flowers.
- Oh dear, I hadn't thought of that.
- But then we'll go to a flower shop.
- That’s always good.
- Yes, that's a good idea.
- Then they can decide what they want.
- Perfect, so we'll do that.

- Kuck mol Monika, wat eis Gaascht eis feel Kadoe matbruecht hunn.
- Jo, an dobäi hatte mir hinnen nach express gesot, si solle mir eis näischt schenken.
- A wéiste wa, deisch menger mir schenken dat alles weider.
- Ma dat ass eng gutt Iddi, a mir invitéiere Frai, denken ech, mir schenken de Kuerisch de Kuss.
- An hei der Schiertes, dat jo ganz flott, mir der schenken deisch mengem Brudder, hier müsst deisch beim Kachen immer knaschtisch.
- Da kanns du him och direkt Kischen, die scho dobäi schenken.
- Jo, an dat Kachbuch hei, dat wir da rappes für denger Mam zu schenken.
- Fällst du do matsoen, dass sie net kache kann?
- Look, Monika, what a guest brought us as a gift.
- Yes, and we had told them not to give us anything.
- And you know what, they might give us everything back.
- Well, that’s a good idea, and we invite Frai, I think we’ll give the Kuerisch a kiss.
- And here the shaved hair, that’s very quick, we give your brother, here you have to be crunchy when cooking.
- Then you can also give him a kiss directly, the one already given.
- Yes, and that cookbook here, we’ll quickly give it to your mom.
- Do you fall there with them that she can’t cook?

- Nee, mee du hues hier scho lang kee Kado méi gemaach.
- Joa, mir sinn jo och net.
- Oh, a kuck hei! E Kalenner mat Kaatze Fotoen!
- Déif hat du jo scho jorelaang net geschenkt.
- Hatt huet sécher gemengt, dée géif mir gefalen.
- Ech géif mengen, nu wënen.
- Joa, si hu jo Fej a Kaatzen.
- Déi gefält dir bestëmmt besonnesch gutt.
- Oh, an hei déi Waass, wiem solle mer déi da schenken?
- Vläicht dem Sophie, hatt ass dach esou frou mat Blummen.
- Gud Idee, mee haut hat eis déi Waass net matbruecht.
- Dat fähe jeng pénibel Situatioun, wa mir hinne se géifen zréckginn.
- No, but you haven't given a card here in a long time.
- Yes, we’re not doing that either.
- Oh, look here! A calendar with cat photos!
- You haven’t given one in ages.
- She surely meant that I would like it.
- I would think so, now they want.
- Yes, they have Fej and cats.
- You’ll surely like those especially well.
- Oh, and here the water, to whom should we give them?
- Perhaps Sophie, she is so happy with flowers.
- Good idea, but today we didn’t bring our water.
- That would put you in a painful situation if we were to take them back.

- Liest an aus stattentäxt iwwer Tröberfauer an den Hämelsmarsch.
- Wéi alljoar zum Schluss vun der grousser Vakanz, freeën sech vill Leit erëm op déi traditionell grouss Kirmes an der Stad.
- Fir vill Leit aus der Maus, ob een emol iwwert Tröberfuer trëppelt, e gebakene Fësch oder en Iesskuch iesst.
- Tröberfauer huet eng laang Geschicht, an ass eng vun deenen eelsten a wichtegsten Traditiounen, déi bis haut nach gefeiert ginn.
- Den 20. Oktober 1343 huet de Jeanne de Blannen, Graf vu Lëtzebuerg a Kinnek vu Bämen, Tröberfauer gegrënnt.
- Am Ufank war et e Joermaart, deen eng Woch gedauert huet.
- Auslännesch Händler sinn op Lëtzebuerg komm, fir ze kafen an ze verkafen.
- Dat war gutt fir d’Economie, awer besonnesch fir den Export vun traditionelle Artikelen wéi Lëtzebuerger Woll.
- Tröberfauer ass traditionell ëmmer den Dag fir de Robertl-Meeschter ugefaangen, also den 23. August.
- Am Ufank ass Tröberfauer um Hällisch-Kiesch-Plato organiséiert ginn.
- Fréier huet dëse Platz Schödpursch geheescht.
- Dohier ass den Numm „Um Schöberfauer“ oder och „Schöbermös“ entstanen.
- Mat der Zäit huet sech Tröberfauer ëmmer weider vergréissert.
- Schon am 17. Joerhonnert ass et op der Lamperzpürsch geplangt ginn an 1893 definitiv op der Glacis.
- Am 20. Joerhonnert koumen ëmmer méi Spiller wéi Trieserat an Dachtabunnen op d’Fouer.
- Lues a lues ass de Spassfaktor op der Fouer ëmmer méi wichteg ginn.
- Mä bis haut fënnt een bei de ville Attraktiounen an an de Restauranten och nach ëmmer Krämerstänn, wou een verschidde Wuer ka kafen.
- Eng Traditioun, déi mat der Schöberfauer verbonnen ass, an generell mat der Kirmes, ass d’Traditioun vum Hämelsmarsch.
- Op der Scheißbunnen oder der Kelebunnen konnt een fréier en Hammel gewannen.
- Fir d’Leit ze weisen, wat si gewanne kënnen, an fir si op d’Kirmes ze invitéieren, ass de Musikverein aus dem Duerf zesumme mam Schofsfjerd duerch d’Stroossen gaangen an huet d’Melodie vum Hämelsmarsch gespillt.
- Och haut gëtt dës Traditioun nach gehalen, och wann et net ëmmer en Hammel dobei gëtt.
- Eent anert Lied, dat haut gespillt gëtt, ass „D’Kirmes am Duerf“.
- Read and listen to the event-text about Tröberfauer in the Hämelsmarsch.
- Every year at the end of the big vacation, many people look forward again to the traditional big fair in the city.
- For many people from the mouse, whether one sometimes steps over Tröberfuer, a baked fish, or a pastry is eaten.
- Tröberfauer has a long history, and is one of the oldest and most important traditions that is still celebrated today.
- On October 20, 1343, Jeanne de Blannen, Count of Luxembourg and King of Bamen, founded Tröberfauer.
- In the beginning it was a one-week market.
- Foreign merchants came to Luxembourg to buy and sell.
- That was good for the economy, but especially for the export of traditional items like Luxembourg wool.
- Tröberfauer traditionally always started on the day for the Robertl-Meeschter, i.e., August 23.
- At first Tröberfauer was organized on the Hällisch-Kiesch-Plato.
- In the past this place was called Schödpursch.
- Afterwards the name “Um Schöberfauer” or also “Schöbermös” emerged.
- Over time Tröberfauer continued to grow.
- Already in the 17th century it was planned on the Lamperzpürsch and definitively on the Glacis in 1893.
- In the 20th century more and more games came to the fair, such as Trieserat and Dachtabunnen.
- Slowly the fun factor at the fair became more important.
- But to this day one still finds market stalls with various goods for sale at the many attractions and in the restaurants.
- A tradition associated with Schöberfauer, and in general with the fair, is the tradition of the Hämelsmarsch.
- On the Scheißbunnen or the Kelebunnen one could formerly win a lamb.
- To show people what they could win and to invite them to the fair, the music association from the village together with the Schofsfjerd walked through the streets and played the melody of the Hämelsmarsch.
- Even today this tradition is kept, even if there is not always a lamb with it.
- Another song that is played today is “The Fair in the Village.”
